title: AUC-hard-negative specialist
summary: AUC is fundamentally pairwise ranking, and imbalance papers show AUC-focused training and hard-negative mining help when easy negatives dominate.
rationale: AUC is fundamentally pairwise ranking, and imbalance papers show AUC-focused training and hard-negative mining help when easy negatives dominate. For this task, the most informative negatives are the non-pit laps that look almost ready to pit.
implementation_hint: Train a specialist on all positives plus weighted hard negatives drawn from high-risk windows: late stint, high degradation, large positive lap-time drift, and laps adjacent to real stops within the same driver-race history. Use pairwise/AUC-oriented optimization or strong cost weighting, then blend the specialist score by rank with the main model.
expected_effect: Improves ordering on ambiguous boundary cases without spending model capacity on obvious stay-out laps.
risk: Too-aggressive mining can overfit niche windows and hurt global robustness if not blended conservatively.
